images.caption: Figure 1:Diagnosis-triggered knowledge-driven planning (horizontal layout).A failed subgoal execution is first analyzed by fine-grained diagnostic signals (e.g., loop detection and unsafe-area failures), then distilled into two reusable knowledge types:guardrailsthat forbid risky actions under specified triggers, andskillsthat summarize successful procedures with explicit preconditions and verification. The retrieved guardrails and skills are injected into the LLM planner to guide safer planning and to trigger local replanning when failures recur, forming a closed-loop of diagnosis, distillation, and knowledge-driven control.

Figure 2:Minecraft technology progression from wooden to diamond tiers,
illustrating the hierarchy of tools, materials, and equipment used to
construct long-horizon tasks for embodied agent evaluation.

Figure 3:Overview of the proposed experience evolution framework for open-world embodied agents.
Embodied interactions are first diagnosed through fine-grained execution signals and recorded as
structured experience documents. A dual-track distillation mechanism then extracts reusable skills
from successful trajectories and guardrails from failure cases. The distilled knowledge is injected
into the LLM planner to guide decision-making and trigger replanning when failures accumulate,
forming a closed-loop process of experience accumulation and knowledge-driven control.

Table 2:Main results on the MCU task suite (Success Rate, SR↑\uparrow, %).
Task abbreviations:Wo.: Wooden,St.: Stone,Ir.: Iron,Go.: Gold,Re.: Redstone,Di.: Diamond,Ar.: Armor.
Best results are highlighted inbold.

Figure 5:(a) Diverse block types form an explorable, harvestable, and modifiable world. (b) The crafting interface illustrates recipe-based item composition and tool progression. (c) Survival mode introduces continuous survival pressure from health, hunger, and environmental hazards. (d) The day–night cycle changes visibility and threats, making exploration and shelter building more strategic.

Figure 6:Case study of failure-aware replanning. A navigation oscillation (NAV_OSCILLATE) is detected near a terrain obstacle, which triggers a new subgoal: break the blocking dirt block before collecting the target log.

Figure 7:Case study of cross-episode learning from failure and success. A failed crafting attempt is distilled into a guardrail, which triggers replanning toward crafting a table first. After success, the completed procedure is further distilled into reusable skill knowledge.
